friction.step: Set "registry" in hyperframes.json to the local committed registry/ path, then run `npx hyperframes add mac-menu-bar`
friction.detail: Pointing `registry` at a local filesystem path (plain absolute path or a `file://` URL) makes every `hyperframes add` fail with `Item "mac-menu-bar" not found — registry unreachable or empty.` — indistinguishable from a typo'd item name. Root cause (found only by reading the installed hyperframes CLI's own bundled source, src/registry/remote.ts): the resolver always does `fetch(`${baseUrl}/registry.json`)`, and Node's fetch has no file:// support, so any non-http(s) registry value silently produces an empty manifest. Real external consumers pasting the README's `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/...` URL never hit this since it's already http(s) — this only bites when validating the registry locally before pushing (a step CONTRIBUTING.md never mentions). Workaround used: `python3 -m http.server` inside `registry/` and point `registry` at `http://127.0.0.1:<port>`.
friction.suggestedFix: Add a short note to CONTRIBUTING.md (or docs/guides/build-hyperframes-demo.md) near the registry section: to test the not-yet-pushed registry locally, serve it (`npx serve registry` or `python3 -m http.server` from `registry/`) and point `hyperframes.json`'s `registry` field at that local URL — filesystem paths are not supported by `hyperframes add`.

friction.step: Render the composed demo (`npx hyperframes render --composition index.html --quality draft --low-memory-mode --output demo.mp4`)
friction.detail: build-hyperframes-demo.md names only `mac-menu-bar` and `claude-chat-pane` as the blocks with the known ~45s "Sub-composition timelines not registered" stall (and says it happens "even with the host flagged"). In the exact three-block composition the docs' own example scene suggests (mac-menu-bar + excel-workbook + claude-chat-pane), the stall instead fired for `excel-workbook` and `claude-chat-pane` — not `mac-menu-bar`, which I'd flagged with `data-no-timeline` per the doc's instruction and which correctly skipped the wait. `excel-workbook` isn't mentioned anywhere as a static/no-timeline block, so there was no way to know in advance it would also cost a 45s stall (render still completed correctly via screenshot fallback, just slower and with a scary-looking log line).
friction.suggestedFix: In docs/guides/build-hyperframes-demo.md, replace the two named exceptions with either a complete, current list of every registry block that registers no timeline (apparently including excel-workbook), or state the general rule: any installed block whose compositions/*.html never calls `window.__timelines[id] = ...` will stall ~45s per host unless flagged `data-no-timeline` — check each block's HTML before assuming it's animated.
